seo_remediation_note: 2026-07-02: SEO build plan Blocks 0, 1, 2, and 3.2 executed, plus N1 (8 missing state hubs) and N2 (repo-wide em/en dash sweep). Block 0 restored the build (was fully broken since Chunk 86). Block 1 made all city pages discoverable (sitemap, internal links, llms.txt). Block 2 removed fabricated review-rating schema, centralized JSON-LD with one stable Organization id, added BreadcrumbList and FAQPage schema where missing, modeled the business as a service-area organization, and removed dead links. Block 3.2 rotated every hard-coded template H2 across 4 variants (src/components/templates/headings.ts) so no H2 exceeds 30% of a template bucket, fixed Template B's H1 to be keyword-forward, and added a build-blocking heading-distribution guard. A word-count guard (scripts/check-word-counts.mjs) found 1,068 of 2,151 city records below their tier word floor; not yet build-blocking, tracked as Block 4. Block 3.1/Block 4 rubric written (Opus): one combined per-record pass for answer-first intros plus body expansion to the tier floor, with a hard no-new-facts YMYL rule; verified 3-record sample (seattle T1 882 words, birmingham T2 595, buda T3 446). Bulk handed to Sonnet against the rubric. New pre-existing finding: banned vocabulary across the corpus (comprehensive x75+), for Block 4's audit. Sonnet bulk 2026-07-02: 16 T1 records (all 3 Arizona T1 plus all 13 California T1). Sonnet bulk 2026-07-03 (batch 2): 13 more T1 records across Colorado, DC, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana. Sonnet bulk 2026-07-03 (batch 3): 15 more T1 records: wichita (KS), louisville and lexington (KY), new-orleans (LA), baltimore (MD), boston (MA), detroit (MI), minneapolis and saint-paul (MN), kansas-city and st-louis (MO), omaha and lincoln (NE), las-vegas and henderson (NV). Sonnet bulk 2026-07-03 (batch 4): 14 more T1 records: newark and jersey-city (NJ), albuquerque (NM), new-york-city and buffalo (NY), charlotte, raleigh, and greensboro (NC), columbus, cleveland, cincinnati, and toledo (OH), oklahoma-city and tulsa (OK). 58 of ~1,051 thin records now done. All T1 records complete from New Jersey through Oklahoma. 18 T1 remain (Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Wisconsin) out of 75 total, then T2 (~273), then T3 (~718). See SEO-BUILD-PLAN-2026-07-02.md changelog for the full per-record word counts and the updated pacing note (next suggested T1 states, the final stretch: Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Wisconsin). Open: continue Block 4 bulk state by state, and Block 3.1 intro review for records not yet touched. Word-count guard is a separate, ongoing effort from the design audit below and is NOT yet resolved (still below floor on remaining T1/T2/T3 records as of 2026-07-03). 2026-07-04 (Opus) trust + technical SEO hardening from a second external audit: softened/removed all unverified trust claims sitewide (fake 4.9 rating, 12,847 reviews, BBB accreditation, 500+ technicians, 50,000+/250K/2.4M homes, years-in-business, NPMA/QualityPro/ISO/OSHA certs, $2M liability), removed the 21 fabricated named testimonials (homepage + residential + reviews page) and rebuilt those sections honestly, softened the absolute technician-licensing claims to fit the lead-gen model, fixed robots.ts to allow /_next/static/, removed misleading per-build sitemap lastmod, dropped the not-live social sameAs links, added Privacy Policy + Terms of Service pages (footer + sitemap), generated placeholder logo.png + og-default.png and wired them into metadata + schema, and removed obsolete keywords metadata. Build clean at 2247 pages, out/ swept clean of fabricated tokens. Follow-ups for Gareth (not code): GSC sitemap submit + URL inspection, Core Web Vitals testing, lawyer review of the legal pages, and collecting real reviews/social profiles before re-introducing either. See SEO-BUILD-PLAN-2026-07-02.md changelog (2026-07-04 entry) for the full file list.
design_remediation_note: 2026-07-03: A browser-based visual design and conversion audit ran against the live site across all 5 city templates plus the core static pages (see DESIGN-BUILD-PLAN-2026-07-03.md for the full plan, decision log, and changelog). Blocks D0 through D6 executed same day and are all complete. D0: fixed 6 sitewide dead service links from the header nav and homepage grid (2 slug typos remapped, 4 real service pages built: rodent-control, skunk-removal, mole-vole-control, commercial-pest-control, services_count is 27, reflected below). D1: fixed a 48-vs-50-states contradiction across roughly 20 page strings, aligned to all 50 states and Washington DC. D2: fixed a crushed data-table column on Templates A and E (roughly 860 pages) caused by a stray whitespace-nowrap. D3: added a sitewide sticky mobile call bar, a 3-field inline lead form on every city template, tightened the homepage hero form, and moved the homepage emergency band from near the bottom of the page to right after the process section. D4: gave Template D a real navy hero (it had none), fixed low-contrast hero pills on A and D, stopped Template E's stat bar from wrapping unevenly, left-aligned ragged centered hero paragraphs on B and C. D5: found and fixed a fabricated company history on the About page (12,000+ technicians, 2.4M+ homes, a named leadership team, an unverifiable Consumer Reports award claim) that contradicted the real site numbers by 20 to 40x; rewrote to the real scale (15+ years, 500+ technicians, 50,000+ homes, no fabricated names or awards) per Gareth's decision, and fixed the same technician-count contradiction on 3 other pages. D6: docs and verification. This design remediation effort is fully closed out, no open items remain from it.
autonomy: Fully autonomous. One block per run (25 pages), full 7-stage pipeline, commit to main, deploy.yml auto-publishes, post live links to Slack as a record. No approval step, no wait-for-go, no stop condition. Advance through the phases in order; when the city universe is built, the pest x city matrix is the long-tail engine and runs indefinitely.
next_chunk_template: mixed (hash-derived per record, not forced per chunk)
tier_t3_note: T3 = every remaining US incorporated place above 5,000 population not already built in T1 or T2. The Census counts roughly 5,000 to 5,300 such places. Build state by state: add one state file per block to src/data/cities/{state}.ts and register it in src/data/cities/index.ts, exactly as T1/T2 were built. 50-state coverage is the target. Chunks 91-92 (2026-07-06, autonomous run, 2 blocks) added 50 T3 records across 10 states (GA, TX, VA, NC, OH for chunk 91 Template D; IL, MI, PA, TN, KS for chunk 92 Template E), all facts web-search-verified by parallel research agents before writing. Chunk 91 caught and fixed 3 duplicate-slug collisions in georgia.ts (forest-park-ga, college-park-ga, chamblee-ga already existed as untracked pre-existing records not reflected in this file's cities_built_list); those 3 were swapped for different, verified, not-yet-built Georgia towns (morrow-ga, clarkston-ga, stone-mountain-ga) rather than overwriting the existing pages. Lesson for future runs: verify candidate slugs against the actual .ts source files (grep for the 4-space-indented `slug: "..."` record-start pattern), not just this JSON's cities_built_list, since the two can drift. Chunks 93-94 (2026-07-07, autonomous run, 2 blocks) added 50 T3 records across 10 more states (AL, IN, IA, LA, MN for chunk 93 Template A; MO, NJ, OK, WI, KY for chunk 94 Template B), 5 towns per state, all facts web-search-verified by parallel research agents before writing. Post-research QA caught and fixed real issues before commit: several agents used invalid serviceSlug values not in src/data/services.ts (fire-ant-treatment, carpenter-ant-control, tick-control, cluster-fly-control all remapped to the real ant-control/flea-tick-treatment/fly-control slugs), HTML-escaped '&' in prose that needed to be a plain '&', one Wisconsin-batch use of the real historical surname 'Foster' (Dwight Foster, Fort Atkinson's 1836 settler; Joel Foster, River Falls' 1848 settler) that collided with the banned-vocabulary word list and had to be rephrased without inventing different names, one new record (eunice-la) that came in under its T3 floor and was expanded using only facts already in its own record, and roughly a dozen nearbyCities links that pointed at towns with no built page yet or at pre-existing thin records (auburn-al, dothan-al, enterprise-al, lafayette-in, kokomo-in, elkhart-in, cedar-rapids-ia, hammond-la, slidell-la, opelousas-la, west-monroe-la, lafayette-la, hannibal-mo, bridgewater-nj, toms-river-nj, ardmore-ok, claremore-ok, shawnee-ok, madison-wi, green-bay-wi, bowling-green-ky, plus a dead easton-pa/lakehurst-nj/merrill-wi/algoma-wi/hudson-wi/new-richmond-wi slug mismatch), all swapped for real, already-built, adequately-worded pages instead. Lesson for future runs: the check:words:changed and check:vocab:changed --changed heuristics scan git-diff-added LINES for any 'slug: "..."' or banned-word match, so a new record's own nearbyCities entries can accidentally pull a same-slug pre-existing record (however thin) into this batch's gate, and a real person's surname can collide with a banned filler word; verify nearbyCities targets actually exist and clear their own floor before relying on them, and check banned-word collisions against real proper nouns before rewriting facts away. Chunks 95-96 (2026-07-08, autonomous run, 2 blocks) added 50 T3 records across 10 more states (FL, AZ, NY, CO, NV for chunk 95 Template C; OR, MD, MA, WA, NM for chunk 96 Template D), 5 towns per state, all facts web-search-verified by 10 parallel research agents before writing. This run hit the documented nearbyCities-pulls-in-thin-pre-existing-record heuristic hard: the whole-corpus word-count backlog is now so large (1,009 of ~2,326 pre-existing records below floor) that roughly 35 of this batch's nearbyCities references initially landed on thin pre-existing pages purely by chance, failing check:words:changed even though every one of the 50 new records cleared its own floor. Fix: computed the full non-thin slug set per state from the whole-corpus report (node scripts/check-word-counts.mjs, no --changed) and re-pointed every flagged nearbyCities entry at either a verified non-thin pre-existing page or, where a state's built pre-existing corpus was almost entirely thin (Nevada: only las-vegas and henderson clear the floor outside this batch), at other new records from this same batch instead. Lesson for future runs: before finalizing nearbyCities on any batch, run the whole-corpus check first and cross-reference candidate targets against it, since the batch-only heuristic cannot warn you in advance and re-diagnosing after a failed gate costs a full extra pass. Also fixed two banned-vocabulary hits this batch introduced directly (Arizona: 'tailored', Oregon: 'when it comes to'); three flagged 'landscape' uses in CO/MA/NV were confirmed literal terrain usage and left as is. Chunks 97-98 (2026-07-11, autonomous run, 2 blocks) added 50 T3 records across 10 more states. Chunk 99 (2026-07-12, autonomous run, 1 block, 25 pages) added 5 T3 towns each to TX (Template A: livingston-tx, orange-tx, marble-falls-tx, alamo-tx, gilmer-tx), CA (Template B: ripon-ca, oakdale-ca, los-banos, placerville, barstow), FL (Template C: bartow, lake-wales, okeechobee, clewiston, palatka), NY (Template D: ballston-spa-ny, malone-ny, penn-yan, wappingers-falls-ny, potsdam-ny), and PA (Template E: emmaus, gettysburg, lititz, bradford-pa, media-pa), all facts web-search-verified by 5 parallel research agents before writing. Ran only 1 block, not 2, this run: research and verification for 25 well-differentiated town records already used the available time well, and quality was prioritized over hitting a 2-block quota. Hit the now well-documented nearbyCities-pulls-in-thin-pre-existing-record issue again (33 of this batch's nearbyCities references initially landed on thin pre-existing pages); fixed by cross-referencing the whole-corpus non-thin slug set per state and re-pointing every flagged reference, in some cases to other new records in this same batch (e.g. Ripon, CA now links to Oakdale, CA and vice versa). Also found and fixed pervasive HTML-escaped '&' in the Texas, California, Florida, New York, and Pennsylvania batches' prose (should be a plain '&' in a TS string, not an HTML entity); this is the second time this exact mistake has shown up in a research-agent batch, worth flagging explicitly to research-agent prompts in future runs. One 'landscape' use in the Marble Falls, TX record ('that limestone landscape') was confirmed literal terrain usage and left as is. npm install had to be run before this session's first build (node_modules was not present in the container). Chunks 104-105 (2026-07-14, autonomous run, 2 blocks) added 50 T3 records across 10 more states, chosen as the 10 states least recently touched by a T3 chunk (IL, KS, MI, TN for chunk 104's oldest-touch group, plus AL, IN, IA, KY, LA, MN): Illinois (mattoon-il, charleston-il, effingham-il, sycamore-il, yorkville-il), Kansas (gardner-ks, el-dorado-ks, colby-ks, concordia-ks, fort-scott-ks), Michigan (wayne, grand-haven, frankenmuth, houghton, howell), Tennessee (pigeon-forge, newport-tn, sparta-tn, bolivar-tn, lenoir-city), Alabama (cullman-al, fairhope-al, bessemer-al, wetumpka-al, sylacauga-al), Indiana (bedford, rushville, angola, tell-city, greencastle), Iowa (carroll-ia, creston-ia, spencer-ia, webster-city-ia, oelwein-ia), Kentucky (harrodsburg-ky, cynthiana-ky, mayfield-ky, maysville-ky, london-ky), Louisiana (deridder, minden, jennings, abbeville, plaquemine), Minnesota (red-wing, albert-lea, worthington, north-mankato, sartell). Ten parallel research-and-write agents (one per state), each editing its own state file directly, all facts web-search-verified, nearbyCities closed-ring per batch with explicit stateSlug, contentTemplate computed via the real slug-hash function. check:vocab:changed caught one real hit (minnesota.ts 'harness' in the Sartell hydropower localHook, rewritten to 'convert Mississippi River flow into hydropower'); four 'landscape' flags across indiana.ts/kansas.ts/louisiana.ts/tennessee.ts were manually confirmed as literal karst/glacial/High-Plains terrain usage and left as is. check:words:changed, check:similarity:changed, and check:headings all passed clean on the first pass after that fix. One pre-existing same-state duplicate slug (texas/burleson, present at HEAD before this session) was found while auditing and left untouched as out of scope, consistent with the chunk 102-103 precedent. npm install had to be run again before this session's build (node_modules was not present in the container). npm run build green at 2641 static pages (2545 city records, up from 2495). Chunks 113-114 (2026-07-18, autonomous run, 2 blocks) added 50 T3 records across 10 states chosen as the least recently touched by a T3 chunk (verified via git log --format=%ci per state file, not just this JSON's drift-prone notes): California, Maryland, Massachusetts, Texas, Washington (chunk 113, last touched at chunks 102-103) and Alabama, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas (chunk 114, last touched at chunks 104-105). Ten parallel research-and-write agents (one per state), each editing its own state file directly. California: banning, reedley, fortuna, auburn, twentynine-palms. Maryland: bethesda-md, catonsville-md, fruitland-md, halfway-md, california-md. Massachusetts: yarmouth, north-adams, ipswich, duxbury, easthampton. Texas: dumas-tx, dripping-springs-tx, henderson-tx, rio-grande-city-tx, port-lavaca-tx. Washington: bainbridge-island, blaine, hoquiam, othello, north-bend. Alabama: demopolis-al, ozark-al, hartselle-al, gulf-shores-al, pell-city-al. Illinois: taylorville-il, mount-vernon-il, antioch-il, beardstown-il, rantoul-il. Indiana: west-lafayette, columbia-city, madison, portland, north-manchester. Iowa: decorah, centerville, orange-city, keokuk, charles-city. Kansas: abilene-ks, augusta-ks, ulysses-ks, pratt-ks, de-soto-ks. All facts web-search-verified before writing; every new record's nearbyCities is a closed ring to the other 4 towns in its own state batch with an explicit stateSlug. Process note: several of the 10 parallel agents stalled after finishing research but before writing the actual records (reporting 'completed' while the target file was unchanged, in a few cases because the agent spawned its own nested research sub-agent and stopped without consuming the result); caught by checking each state file's record count against its pre-batch baseline immediately after each completion notification, and resumed via SendMessage (pasting the gathered research directly where needed) rather than accepting the stall. Lesson for future runs: always verify a completed agent's target file actually changed before trusting its written self-report. contentTemplate/contentSoul were written as literal 'PLACEHOLDER'/'placeholder' strings by every agent, then corrected in one centralized, scoped script keyed off each record's own slug (matching only the literal placeholder markers, never touching any pre-existing record), verified via git diff --numstat showing pure additions (0 deletions) across all 10 files both before and after the fix ran. check:vocab:changed flagged 8 'landscape' uses (4 Iowa, 1 Kansas, 3 Maryland), all manually reviewed against the diff and confirmed literal terrain/geography usage (wooded/pastured landscape, limestone karst landscape, landscaped Eisenhower Library grounds, ponds spread across the landscape), none rewritten. check:words:changed, check:similarity:changed, and check:headings all passed clean. npm install had to be run (fresh container, no node_modules). npm run build green at 2847 static pages (2751 city records, up from 2701).

pest_city_matrix._goal: THE LONG-TAIL ENGINE. A page for each pest service in each city: '{service} in {city}, {state}' (for example 'Bed Bug Treatment in Austin, TX'). This is exactly how the market searches and is the highest-volume long-tail layer on the site. 25 services x cities. Sized to the priority-city set first, not all 5,000 towns.
pest_city_matrix.route_to_build: New dynamic route, e.g. src/app/locations/[slug]/[city]/[service]/page.tsx (service x city x state), or src/app/[service]/[slug]/[city]/page.tsx. Reuse the 5 city templates A-E and the service data in src/data/services.ts. generateStaticParams crosses services x the chosen city set.
pest_city_matrix.rollout: Phase 6, after the city universe is substantial. Start with the highest-urgency, highest-volume services (bed-bug-treatment, cockroach-control, termite-treatment, mice-control, rat-control, mosquito-control, ant-control, wasp-bee-removal) across the 75 T1 cities, then widen services, then widen cities. One block = one service across the next 25 cities. Skip-check the generated path before building.
pest_city_matrix.sizing: 25 services x 400 priority cities = ~10,000 pages at full build. Sized to demand: do not build all 25 services for all 5,000 towns. Build the full 25 x T1 (75 cities) = 1,875, then the 8 priority services x T2 (300) = 2,400, then extend by demand.

blog_plan.note: Phase 5. Seasonal and pest-specific articles (e.g. 'when do termites swarm', 'are stink bugs harmful'), built 5 per batch once the city and pest x city engines are running. Non-cannibalising: blog covers questions the service and service-city pages do not.

phase_order: Phase 2: T2 COMPLETE (300 cities, 75 T1 + 225 T2 built before chunk 15, +25 T2 in chunk 15 = 300 T2 total).
Phase 3: T3 town universe (~5,000 incorporated places >5,000 pop), state by state, 25 per block. 225 built in chunks 16-24.
Phase 6: pest x city matrix (the long-tail engine), one service x 25 cities per block.
Phase 5: blog plan (200 articles), 5 per batch, interleaved once the engines run.
Phase 4: towns below 5,000 population, only if T1-T3 index well and demand justifies it.
build_order_each_run: Default: build the next 25-city block of the current phase (T3 now). Once T3 is materially complete, the pest x city matrix becomes the default block. Blog batches run only when the next city/matrix block is already committed.
notes: Chunks 106-107 (2026-07-15, autonomous run, Sonnet) added 50 T3 records across 10 more states, chosen as the 10 states least recently touched by a T3 chunk (MO, WI, NJ, OK not touched since chunk 94; CO, NM, SC, CT, UT since chunk 95-97; OR since chunk 96): Missouri (Maryville, Moberly, Marshall, West Plains, Mexico), Wisconsin (Platteville, Marinette, Ashland, Portage, Chippewa Falls), New Jersey (Morristown, Cranford, Bridgeton, Point Pleasant, Somerville), Oklahoma (Weatherford, Sallisaw, Grove, Poteau, Purcell), Colorado (Fort Morgan, Craig, Breckenridge, Trinidad, Superior), Oregon (Astoria, The Dalles, Silverton, Baker City, Cottage Grove), New Mexico (Sunland Park, Bernalillo, Socorro, Grants, Truth or Consequences), South Carolina (North Augusta, Georgetown, Clemson, Camden, Walterboro), Connecticut (Wallingford, Southington, Willimantic, Old Saybrook, Simsbury), Utah (Park City, Brigham City, Price, Richfield, Moab). Ten parallel research-and-write agents (one per state), each editing its own state file directly, all facts web-search-verified, nearbyCities closed-ring per batch with explicit stateSlug. Post-agent QA caught a real process bug: several agents computed contentTemplate/contentSoul by hand or via ad hoc node one-liners and got roughly half of them wrong versus the real variantForSlug hash; a scoped correction script fixed all 50 to the correct hash-derived values. That correction script's first two draft versions had a serious bug of their own (matching every record in a file, not just the 5 new ones, via an unanchored slug search) and briefly overwrote the deliberately hand-balanced contentTemplate/contentSoul values on hundreds of pre-existing records across all 10 files; caught before commit by diffing against HEAD (git diff --numstat showed large unexpected deletions instead of pure additions), and fixed by rebuilding each file from pristine HEAD content plus only the 5 new records appended, each corrected to its real hash value. Final diff for all 10 files is a pure, clean addition versus HEAD (0 lines deleted except one blank-line normalization in south-carolina.ts). Lesson for future runs: never run a template/soul verification pass with a slug-matching regex that is not explicitly scoped to the known list of new slugs for that batch, and always confirm a fix-up script's diff is pure-addition before trusting it. check:vocab:changed flagged 2 landscape uses (oklahoma.ts sallisaw-ok, south-carolina.ts camden-sc), both manually confirmed literal terrain/geography usage (forested Cookson Hills terrain; the Sandhills dune terrain) and left as is. check:words:changed, check:similarity:changed, and check:headings all passed clean. npm install had to be run (fresh container, no node_modules). npm run build green at 2691 static pages (2595 city records, up from 2545). Template distribution held balanced: A=529 (20.4%), B=497 (19.2%), C=534 (20.6%), D=518 (20.0%), E=517 (19.9%), all within the 15-25% target. Chunks 108-109 (2026-07-15, autonomous run, second run of the day, Sonnet) added 50 T3 records across 10 more states, chosen as the 10 states least recently touched by a T3 chunk (by last-commit date per state file): Alaska (Wasilla, Kenai, Kodiak, Sitka, Ketchikan), Hawaii (Kapolei, Kihei, Kahului, Wahiawa, Waianae), Arkansas (Marion, De Queen, Pea Ridge, Arkadelphia, Monticello), Idaho (Emmett, Weiser, Fruitland, Hailey, Shelley), Mississippi (Madison, Indianola, D'Iberville, Senatobia, Petal), Nebraska (Gretna, Ralston, Plattsmouth, Crete, Schuyler), West Virginia (South Charleston, Nitro, Dunbar, Hurricane, Ranson), Florida (Mount Dora, Milton, Live Oak, Immokalee, Pahokee), New York (New City, Mamaroneck, Seneca Falls, Herkimer, Fredonia), Pennsylvania (Horsham, Northampton, Columbia, Elizabethtown, Sayre). Ten parallel research-and-write agents (one per state), each editing its own state file directly, all facts web-search-verified (University/Extension sources, Census/ACS population data, verified local geography and industry facts), nearbyCities closed-ring per batch with explicit stateSlug. Learning applied from the chunks 106-107 process bug: every agent was instructed to write contentTemplate: "PLACEHOLDER" and contentSoul: "placeholder" as literal strings rather than hand-computing the hash, and a single centralized script (run once, after all 10 agents finished) replaced exactly those 50 known new-record placeholders with the real variantForSlug hash output, verified via git diff --numstat showing pure additions (0 deletions) both before and after the fix ran. Alaska correctly excludes termite claims (confirmed via research that Alaska's climate does not support the termite species active in the Lower 48); Hawaii correctly emphasizes coconut rhinoceros beetle, Formosan termites, and centipedes per University of Hawaii CTAHR and Hawaii Dept of Agriculture sourcing; Arkansas cross-checked the federal fire-ant quarantine county list before attaching fire-ant claims per town. check:vocab:changed flagged 15 "landscape"/"landscaped" uses in hawaii.ts, all manually reviewed and confirmed literal (terrain/land or actual planted/irrigated vegetation), none of the banned figurative-filler sense, left as is. check:words:changed, check:similarity:changed, and check:headings all passed clean. npm install had to be run (fresh container, no node_modules). npm run build green at 2741 static pages (2645 city records, up from 2595). Template distribution held balanced: A=540 (20.4%), B=505 (19.1%), C=544 (20.6%), D=527 (19.9%), E=529 (20.0%), all within the 15-25% target. Chunks 110-111 (2026-07-16, autonomous run, Sonnet) targeted the 10 states least recently touched by a T3 chunk (DE, VT, ME, NH, RI, NV, MT, SD, ND, WY, last touched at chunks 100-101). Ten parallel research-and-write agents (one per state) researched real, current population figures against every candidate place before writing anything. Result: 31 new pages, well short of the 50-page/2-block target, because this particular 10-state group is the same small-population bucket used in chunks 100-101, and three of those states (South Dakota, North Dakota, Wyoming) were already confirmed exhausted of every real incorporated place or CDP over the 5,000 population floor at that prior run and remain exhausted now (each agent re-verified with fresh Census/ACS figures and added zero pages, reporting exactly what was checked). Montana yielded only 1 new page (Helena Valley West Central CDP) for the same reason, real capacity nearly gone. The other six states cleared their full 5-town quota: Delaware (Bear, Hockessin, Claymont, Pike Creek, Brookside, all New Castle County CDPs), Vermont (Essex, Northfield, Waterbury, Jericho, Morristown/Morrisville), Maine (Kennebunk, Yarmouth, Winslow, Rockland, Skowhegan), New Hampshire (Berlin, Franklin, Lebanon, Newmarket, Raymond), Rhode Island (Smithfield, Lincoln, Portsmouth, Middletown, Tiverton), and Nevada (Paradise, Spring Valley, Sunrise Manor, Enterprise, Whitney, all large unincorporated Las Vegas Valley CDPs, deliberately written to read as distinct from each other and from the existing Las Vegas page rather than templated). No pages were padded and no population floor was lowered anywhere; the shortfall is reported plainly per the routine's anti-padding rule. contentTemplate/contentSoul were written as literal PLACEHOLDER/placeholder strings by every agent, then a single centralized Node script (scoped strictly to the 31 known new slugs, verified via git diff --numstat showing pure additions with 0 deletions across all 7 changed files before AND after the fix) replaced them with the real variantForSlug hash output, continuing the safe process established in chunks 108-109. check:words:changed caught 3 issues on the first pass: two were the well-documented nearbyCities-pulls-in-a-thin-pre-existing-page heuristic (a new Nevada record's ring referenced the pre-existing but thin north-las-vegas, T2 at 273 words; a new Rhode Island record's ring referenced the pre-existing but thin central-falls-ri), both fixed by re-pointing to verified non-thin pre-existing pages (henderson and north-providence respectively, confirmed clean via a state-scoped whole-corpus check:words pass) rather than touching the thin pre-existing records themselves; the third was a genuine issue in this batch's own content, one new Vermont record (northfield-vt) landed 24 words under its 350-word T3 floor, fixed by expanding its Dog River valley/tick/mosquito section with a real yellow-jacket paragraph built entirely from a fact already present in that record's own pestProfile data, not a new claim. check:vocab:changed, check:similarity:changed, and check:headings all passed clean after that fix. npm install had to be run (fresh container, no node_modules). npm run build green at 2772 static pages (2676 city records, up from 2645). Template distribution held balanced: A=547 (20.4%), B=512 (19.1%), C=549 (20.5%), D=533 (19.9%), E=535 (20.0%), all within the 15-25% target. Next: chunk 112, T3 continues; since this small-state bucket is now confirmed to have very little real capacity left (SD/ND/WY fully exhausted, MT and RI and DE nearly there), future least-recently-touched rotations reaching this same 10-state group should expect a similarly small yield and should not force a 50-page target against it; a future session may want to formally mark SD, ND, and WY as T3-complete rather than re-checking them every rotation. Chunk 112 (2026-07-16, autonomous run, second run of the day, Sonnet) targeted the 4 states least recently touched by a T3 chunk (GA, VA, NC, OH, last touched at chunk 91-92) plus the next-oldest (AZ, last touched at chunk 95). Built only 1 block (25 pages), not 2, this run: research and verification for 5 states already used the available time well, and quality was prioritized over a second block. check:words:changed caught the nearbyCities-pulls-in-a-thin-pre-existing-page heuristic again, this time hitting harder than usual: the 4 'safe' established-city anchors assumed for VA (richmond, norfolk, virginia-beach, roanoke), 3 of 4 assumed for GA (columbus, savannah, macon; atlanta held), and 1 of 5 assumed for AZ (scottsdale; phoenix/tucson/mesa held) turned out to already be below their own tier floor in the pre-existing corpus, plus one NC batch record linked the pre-existing but thin durham and one OH batch record linked the pre-existing but thin dayton. Fixed all of these (5 Virginia records, 2 North Carolina records, 1 Ohio record) by re-pointing to other new records in the same batch's closed ring instead, verified clean via a second check:words:changed pass. Lesson for future runs: do not assume a T1/T2 city is automatically clear of the word-count floor just because it is a major, long-built metro; the whole-corpus word-count backlog (Block 4, tracked separately) includes some large cities too, so always verify a proposed nearbyCities anchor against a fresh check:words pass (not just its tier) before treating it as safe, or default straight to an all-batch closed ring. check:vocab:changed, check:similarity:changed, and check:headings all passed clean after the nearbyCities fix. npm install had to be run (fresh container, no node_modules). npm run build green at 2797 static pages (2701 city records, up from 2676).
